title: Lo Khan
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Lo}} {{Character |type=Criminal |image=[[File:LoKhan-DE5.jpg]] |name=Lo Khan |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]][[Dark Empire 5|''Dark Empire'' 5]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Lo Khan's on the beam. He knows ''spacers'' gotta stick together in this crazy galaxy!|[[Shug Ninx]]|}} '''Lo Khan''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] who was active during the early years of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Khan was a calm individual and tried to stay on the safer side of smuggling, avoiding dispute and armed conflict whenever possible, and spending time joking and drinking with his friends inside [[Cantina/Legends|cantinas]] during his free time. As a smuggler, he made the most profitable run of his life on the [[Gamor Run/Legends|Gamor Run]], where he stole a [[Spice/Legends|spice]] shipment belonging to one of his rivals, [[Uxbeg]]. After stealing it, he delivered the payload to a [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] named [[Spadda]] in the [[Aikhibba system/Legends|Aikhibba system]]. Along the way, he teamed up with [[Luwingo]], a [[Yaka]] [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] who was having trouble finding work. Luwingo saved Khan's life in a shootout with Uxbeg's henchmen. This impressed Khan enough to hire the Yaka as his full-time [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]], thus cementing what was to become a long and fruitful partnership. Khan earned enough [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]] from that adventure to semi-retire himself, spending the following years inside cantinas all around [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] while doing various smuggling runs in his [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]], the ''[[Hyperspace Marauder]]''. In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], while docked at the [[Imperial Freight Complex]] around [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], Khan ran into a couple of his old smuggling friends, [[Salla Zend]] and [[Shug Ninx]], who were fleeing pursuing Imperial forces. Khan agreed to let Zend and Ninx store the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] the two fugitive smugglers were borrowing from former [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], inside the ''Marauder''{{'s}} cargo hold. As Zend and Ninx prepared to leave, an Imperial [[Hunter-Killer probot]] located the ''Falcon'' inside the ''Marauder'' and fired on Khan's ship in an attempt to apprehend the rogue vessel, much to the smuggler's dismay. The ''Marauder'' was impounded by the Imperials, stranding Khan and Luwingo on Byss for the next few weeks. Eventually, Khan and a group of smugglers were able to take advantage of a secret New Republic [[Battle of the Emperor's Citadel|attack]] on Byss to retrieve their ships from the Imperial impound. Khan and the newly formed smuggler [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] aided the New Republic forces in escaping the doomed mission to besiege the [[Emperor's Citadel]] just as the tide was turning in the Empire's favor. The group then proceeded to assist in the [[Battle of New Alderaan (Operation Shadow Hand)|evacuation]] of [[New Alderaan/Legends|New Alderaan]] after the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] was attacked by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] forces. ==Biography== ===Smuggling career=== {{Quote|Keep it to yourself, but ''Luwingo'' here saved my can in a shootout with Imperials on the Gamor Run. I keep him around for ''protection''.|Lo Khan to Shug Ninx|}} [[File:Luwingo_swenc.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Luwingo, Lo Khan's long-time partner and companion]] Lo Khan was a seasoned, laconic [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] who was known to have performed difficult smuggling runs since before the rise of such infamous criminals as [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]]. Khan made the largest haul of his smuggling career on the [[Gamor Run/Legends|Gamor Run]],''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' a [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperspace route]] in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' Khan had learned that one of his smuggling rivals, [[Uxbeg]], had been contracted to transport a [[Spice/Legends|spice]] shipment to a [[Crime lord/Legends|criminal]] named [[Spadda]]. Uxbeg was still busy running a load of bulk [[yeast paste]] to the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]] [[Prison/Legends|prison]] [[Stars' End/Legends|Stars' End]], allowing Khan an opportunity to craftily intercept Uxbeg's pending shipment. Upon acquiring the shipment, Khan was surprised to learn that it was a spice consignment from the royal [[Governor/Legends|governor]] of the [[Thokosia system/Legends|Thokosia system]] destined for the [[Aikhibba system/Legends|Aikhibba system]], a minor stopping points along the Gamor Run. Khan was already running low on [[Fuel/Legends|fuel]] and, deciding to take the shipment immediately, departed rather than give his rival ample time to arrive and pick up the shipment himself. Stopping in the [[Deneba system/Legends|Deneba system]] to refuel, Khan encountered a [[Yaka]] [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] that he had met on an earlier run known as [[Luwingo]]. Luwingo was despondent about the state of his affairs, and Khan—sympathizing with the Yaka, as he had been in similar situations before—decided to take Luwingo with him and give him a small cut of the profit to which Khan never demanded any payback. Soon, Khan and Luwingo had a run-in with a group of Uxbeg's thugs. The skirmish degenerated into a [[Lightfight|firefight]] that resulted in Luwingo taking a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] [[Laser/Legends|bolt]] to protect Khan. Khan feared that Luwingo was [[Death/Legends|dead]], but to his amazement, the Yaka simply shook off the injury. It was then that Khan became aware of Luwingo's usefulness and loyalty, and so decided to hire Luwingo as his full-time [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]]. Upon delivering the cargo to [[Aikhibba]], Khan was shocked to learn that Spadda was the infamous [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] "Spadda the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]]" and became disappointed when the Hutt shortchanged him immensely. Nevertheless, Khan still received a significant sum that enabled him to spend the following years relaxing in [[Cantina/Legends|smugglers' bars]] across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] while performing small runs in his modified [[Xiytiar-class heavy transport/Legends|''Xiytiar''-class transport]], the ''[[Hyperspace Marauder]]'', at his leisure. At some point during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], he began [[Deep Core hauler|hauling military supplies]] to the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] for both sides of the war.''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' ===Aiding the New Republic=== {{Quote|Ah, you're a lifesaver, Lo Khan. Thanks for letting us hide the ''Millennium Falcon'' inside your ship.''"
"''Yeah, Ninx, the ''Hyperspace Marauder''{{'s}} got room. But both of you owe me big. If the Imperials find out, I'm dead meat.|Shug Ninx and Lo Khan|audio=LoKhan2.flac|[[Dark Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Empire'' audio drama]]}} [[File:Marauder-Probot.jpg|thumb|250px|The Hunter-Killer probot Judgement 12-X7 locates the ''Millennium Falcon'' inside the ''Hyperspace Marauder''{{'s}} cargo hold.]] By [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] had [[Transfer essence|resurrected himself]] in a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] body and began constructing a revived [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] based around the Deep Core [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Byss/Legends|Byss]]. [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] heroes [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] and former smuggler Han Solo enlisted the help of smugglers [[Salla Zend]] and [[Shug Ninx]] in their attempt to rescue Leia's brother and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], who had recently been captured by the forces of the new Empire. The group traveled in Solo's [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] to the [[Emperor's Citadel]] on Byss, where Han and Leia allowed themselves to be captured as part of their plan to rescue Skywalker while Zend and Ninx escaped with the ''Falcon''.[[Dark Empire 4|''Dark Empire'' 4]] Zend and Ninx fled to the [[Imperial Freight Complex]] on Byss, where Lo Khan allowed his fellow smugglers to conceal the ''Falcon'' inside the ''Hyperspace Marauder''. Khan, while willing to help his fellow smugglers in need, was also concerned about the well-being of both his [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] and his own life. As Khan, Luwingo, and the two fugitive smugglers discussed recent events at the complex's cantina, the [[Byss Bistro]], Ninx received a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] transmission from Han Solo, who requested extraction from the Citadel. As Zend and Ninx headed back toward the ''Falcon'', an Imperial [[Hunter-Killer probot]] designated [[Judgement 12-X7]] scanned the ''Marauder'' and discovered the ''Falcon'' inside the ship's cargo hold. The probot was able to capture the ''Falcon'' with its [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beams]] but only after it crippled and impounded the ''Marauder''. Khan, was unworried, however, as he felt confident that he could use his connections to free the ''Marauder''. It was not long before Lo Khan received a chance to recover his ship. For the next few weeks, Khan and Luwingo found themselves stranded on Byss, where they once again met up with Salla Zend and Shug Ninx at the Byss Bistro.[[Dark Empire II 4|''Dark Empire II'' 4]] The two smugglers came back to Byss, intending to salvage Zend's ship, the ''[[Starlight Intruder]]'', and were able to get past Imperial security by posing as maintenance workers delivering a set of obsolete repair parts to Khan.[[Dark Empire II 3|''Dark Empire II'' 3]] Khan revealed, much to Zend's disappointment, that the ''Intruder'' had already been stripped by Imperial-sanctioned [[Scavenger/Legends|scavs]] and melted to slag. Khan's break finally came in the form of a [[Battle of the Emperor's Citadel|surprise attack]] by [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] forces inside [[X-1 Viper]] Automadons led by [[General/Legends|Generals]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] and Lando Calrissian. Soon, however, the Imperials unleashed their [[chrysalide]] rancors, [[Sith alchemy/Legends|alchemically]] [[Mutant|mutated]] [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[Sithspawn/Legends|beasts]] on the Viper Automadons. As things began to look grim for the New Republic, Salla Zend, feeling that she had a responsibility to them, rallied Khan and many other smugglers together to aid the New Republic forces.[[Dark Empire II 5|''Dark Empire II'' 5]] Luwingo laid down covering fire with his [[tripod laser]] as Khan and the other [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] rushed to their impounded ships. With Luwingo safely on board, Khan lifted off in the ''Hyperspace Marauder'' and joined the small [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of smuggler ships. Khan made use of the ''Marauder''{{'s}} newly-installed [[700-Penetrator]] [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] in conjunction with the other smuggler ships to save Antilles, Calrissian, and the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] from a chrysalide rancor. Khan and the smuggler ships escaped Byss's [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] and were surprised when the Imperials allowed them to reach a jumping distance without firing on them—for fear of damaging the nearby, partially-constructed [[Galaxy Gun]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]]. The smuggler ships safely escaped to the New Republic–held [[Pinnacle Base]]. Shortly thereafter, Khan and the smuggler ships helped [[Battle of New Alderaan (Operation Shadow Hand)|evacuate]] the New Republic [[Safe world/Legends|safe world]] of [[New Alderaan/Legends|New Alderaan]], where Leia Organa Solo's children, [[Darth Caedus|Jacen]] and [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]], were hiding out, before finally heading off to the New Republic base at the [[Nespis VIII|Space City]] orbiting [[Nespis VIII]].[[Dark Empire II 6|''Dark Empire II'' 6]] ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|…So I says, 'Thrusters? I got your thrusters right here, baby!'|Lo Khan jokes with his smuggler friends at the Byss Bistro|audio=LoKhan1.flac|}} [[File:Byss_Bistro.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Lo Khan often frequented the Byss Bistro with his smuggling companions.]] Lo Khan was an easy-going, laconic smuggler who had gained a reputation as one of the most lax in his trade. He spent much of his free time becoming drunk in cantinas with old friends, all the while occasionally performing small, safe runs in the ''Hyperspace Marauder'' to make a living. Khan was one of the older smugglers of his time and preferred to keep out of trouble. He typically kept to the more well-policed routes and spent most of his [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]] on upgrading the [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] and [[Hull/Legends|hull]] of his ship, for which he owned a [[Beckon call/Legends|beckon call]]. Khan liked to avoid conflicts and preferred to make few enemies, so he kept the ''Marauder'' unarmed for many years before finally deciding to arm it with twin 700-Penetrator laser cannons. If the ''Marauder'' was ever taken over by [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], the ship's [[Computer/Legends|computer]] was programmed to override the computer on the pirating vessel and give Khan complete control of all of the ship's systems. Khan was not above mercy, however, and was willing to leave life support and low-level [[Ion engine/Legends|ion thrusters]] operational before stripping any valuable gear from the pirate ship. While hauling in the Deep Core, he could usually be found sitting in the Byss Cantina with his fellow smugglers. Khan was also willing to help a friend in need, so long as he or his ship did not get damaged in the process. Khan formed a long-standing friendship and partnership with his Yaka cyborg [[First Mate/Legends|first mate]], Luwingo, beginning with their first run together. ==Behind the scenes== Lo Khan was created by author [[Tom Veitch]] for the [[Dark Empire 5|fifth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series. He later returned for the latter half of the sequel series, ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire II]]'', and was given a complete backstory by [[Michael Allen Horne]] in the ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]''. He was also given brief mentions in various other sources over the years. He was voiced by [[Joe Hacker]] in the [[Dark Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Empire'']] and [[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|''Dark Empire II'' audio adaptations]].[[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|''Dark Empire II'' audio drama]] Though the fifth issue of ''Dark Empire'' indicates that Khan was saved by Luwingo during a firefight against Imperials, the ''Dark Empire Sourcebook'' instead identifies the attackers as hirelings of Uxbeg. This article assumes that the ''Dark Empire Sourcebook'', being the most recent and comprehensive source on the subject, is correct. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|soundcat=Audio files of Lo Khan|imagecat=Images of Lo Khan}} *[[Dark Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Empire'' audio drama]] *[[Dark Empire 5|''Dark Empire'' 5]] {{1st}} *[[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|''Dark Empire II'' audio drama]] *[[Dark Empire II 3|''Dark Empire II'' 3]] {{Mo}} *[[Dark Empire II 4|''Dark Empire II'' 4]] *[[Dark Empire II 5|''Dark Empire II'' 5]] *[[Dark Empire II 6|''Dark Empire II'' 6]] {{C|Indirect appearance}} ==Sources== *''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' {{C|Under ''Shug Ninx'' and ''Salla Zend''}} *''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' {{C|Under ''Hyperspace Marauder''}} *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *[[Star Wars Handbook 3: Dark Empire|''Star Wars Handbook'' 3: ''Dark Empire'']] {{C|Under ''Salla Zend/Shug Ninx''}} *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' {{C|Under ''Hyperspace Marauder''}} *{{FactFile|93|BYS 1-2|Byss}} {{Po}} *{{FactFile|116|CAL 19-22|Lando Calrissian}} {{Imo}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|sallazend|Zend, Salla}} *{{FFGTCG|set=Knowledge and Defense|cardname=''Hyperspace Marauder''|link=echoes-of-the-force-cycle/knowledge-and-defense/hyperspace-marauder-knowledge-and-defense-109-2}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Khan, Lo}} [[Category:Individuals of unidentified species]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Smugglers]]
